title: abronia umbellata
intro: Abronia umbellata, also known as the San Gabriel Island sand verbena, is a species of flowering plant in the family Nyctaginaceae. It is a shrubby perennial herb that grows in coastal scrub and sandy areas. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and soothe digestive issues. Abronia umbellata is often prepared as an herbal tea, salve, or tincture, and is valued for its health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial properties.
